paragraph: Fairview's single-family market is moving at a measured pace with 8 sales over six months at a median of $617,500. Homes are selling slightly under asking at a 0.97 sale-to-list ratio, with a median of 22 days on market. Only about 37 percent are fetching over asking, which tells you sellers need to price competitively to move inventory. Multi-family properties, by contrast, are the real strength here. Twelve sales landed at a median of $817,500, and buyers are actively bidding these properties up, with 75 percent selling above asking and a sale-to-list ratio of 1.04. The median days on market of 20.5 reflects genuine buyer interest in the two to three unit space. Condos and townhouses sit at the lower price point with 7 sales at a median of $545,000, but they're spending more time on the market, averaging 34 days. Only 14 percent are crossing the finish line above asking, and they're selling at a 0.98 ratio, suggesting buyer caution in this tier. If you're comparing property types in Fairview, the deciding factor isn't market heat. It's whether you want the space and grounds of a single-family or multi-family investment, or the lower price point of a condo or townhouse.
